content.text: Action Item Ownership and Closure SLAs

Every postmortem action item is a commitment: the team acknowledges a systemic gap and assigns responsibility for closing it. An action item without an owner, a deadline, or a completion criterion is a hypothesis — it may or may not get done. The action item ownership and SLA system converts hypotheses into accountable commitments.

Closure SLAs create a clock that makes the organisation's reliability ambition visible. When action items consistently close on time, the postmortem process is working. When they systematically miss deadlines, the postmortem process is producing work that is not being resourced — a more useful signal than a high incident count.

attrs.alt: Action item lifecycle state machine: OPEN transitions to IN_PROGRESS on assignment, IN_PROGRESS to BLOCKED when blocked, BLOCKED back to IN_PROGRESS when unblocked, either to CLOSED on completion, and both OPEN and IN_PROGRESS transition to OVERDUE when the SLA deadline passes.

content.text: Every action item is a structured record with mandatory and optional fields. The mandatory fields are required before an action item can be marked OPEN — without them, the item is a note, not a commitment.

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, date
from enum import Enum

class ActionItemState(str, Enum):
    OPEN        = "open"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    BLOCKED     = "blocked"
    OVERDUE     = "overdue"
    CLOSED      = "closed"

class ActionItemCategory(str, Enum):
    DETECTION   = "detection"    # add or improve an alert
    PREVENTION  = "prevention"   # change code/config to prevent recurrence
    RUNBOOK     = "runbook"      # document a missing or inadequate procedure
    PROCESS     = "process"      # change an organisational practice

@dataclass
class ActionItem:
    # Mandatory fields
    id: str                       # PM-{incident_id}-{seq}, e.g. PM-2025-0847-01
    title: str                    # one-sentence description of the deliverable
    postmortem_id: str
    category: ActionItemCategory
    owner: str                    # individual name, not team
    target_date: date
    completion_criterion: str     # how we know it is done

    # Optional
    state: ActionItemState = ActionItemState.OPEN
    created_at: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)
    closed_at: datetime | None = None
    blocked_reason: str | None = None
    escalation_owner: str | None = None  # EM or senior IC if overdue

    def is_overdue(self, as_of: date | None = None) -> bool:
        check_date = as_of or date.today()
        return self.state not in (ActionItemState.CLOSED,) and check_date > self.target_date

    def days_overdue(self, as_of: date | None = None) -> int:
        if not self.is_overdue(as_of):
            return 0
        check_date = as_of or date.today()
        return (check_date - self.target_date).days
SLA targets are defined by action item category, not by incident severity. A detection improvement for a P1 incident carries the same urgency as one from a P0 — the systemic gap is the same.
Monitoring gaps leave the system blind to recurrence; close first
Code and config changes require engineering time; balanced against urgency
Documentation requires no deployment; fastest to close
Process changes need coordination; some require leadership approval

The 'Hard Escalation' column means the Engineering Manager receives an automatic notification and must provide a written disposition within 48 hours: extend with a new date, reassign, or accept and document the residual risk. Ignoring a hard escalation triggers a director-level notification.

An action item owner is a named individual, not a team. 'Platform team owns this' means no one owns it. If the work genuinely belongs to a team, the team's Engineering Manager is the owner until they assign it to an individual.
Bob Zhao, this sprint's rotation lead
Dan Kim — reassign when IC rotates

A postmortem action item is not valid until the owner has explicitly acknowledged it. The acknowledgement can be a Slack reaction, a Notion comment, or a GitHub issue assignment — whatever the team uses — but it must be traceable. An unacknowledged action item reverts to OPEN status.

An action item is not CLOSED when the owner says it is done. It is CLOSED when the completion criterion is verifiably satisfied. The completion criterion is written at the time the action item is created, not retroactively.
Alert 'inference-gpu-memory-high' firing in staging at 80% threshold, linked in PagerDuty runbook
PR #4821 merged: classifier rollout gated on refusal-rate-delta < 5% in CI; test visible in CI config
Runbook 'weight-server-rollout' updated with GPU memory alert check step, reviewed by on-call lead, linked from Notion page
Classifier change approval form updated to include safety review checkbox; form posted to #platform-oncall; confirmed in use for next 3 changes

The Engineering Manager or a designated reviewer — not the owner — verifies that the completion criterion is satisfied before marking the item CLOSED. This prevents self-certification where the owner declares something done without meeting the criterion.
Item transitions to OVERDUE; owner notified via Slack DM
Acknowledge and provide updated target date
EM notified; item appears in weekly digest overdue list
Written update: status + new date or blocker identification
EM receives escalation notification; must respond within 48h
EM provides disposition: extend, reassign, or accept residual risk
Director-level notification; item flagged in quarterly review
EM and Director align on resolution path

A blocked action item is distinct from an overdue one. An item is blocked when the owner has identified a specific external dependency preventing progress — a third-party approval, a dependency on another team's work, or a budget gate. Blocked items require an updated target date and a named blocker.
Owner is actively working on it
Document blocker, estimate unblock date, escalate if needed
SLA clock paused if blocker accepted by EM
Target date passed without closure or BLOCKED transition
Immediate written update; request extension or reassignment

'I have not had time to work on it' is not a valid blocked reason. It means the item is OVERDUE, not BLOCKED. BLOCKED is reserved for genuine external dependencies that are outside the owner's control.

def block_action_item(
    action_item: ActionItem,
    blocked_reason: str,
    estimated_unblock_date: date,
    em_acknowledged: bool,
) -> ActionItem:
    VALID_BLOCKER_PATTERNS = [
        "waiting for approval from",
        "depends on",
        "blocked by external",
        "pending third-party",
        "infrastructure access",
    ]
    reason_lower = blocked_reason.lower()
    if not any(pat in reason_lower for pat in VALID_BLOCKER_PATTERNS):
        raise ValueError(
            "Blocked reason must describe an external dependency. "
            "'No time' or 'busy' are not valid blocked reasons."
        )
    if not em_acknowledged:
        raise PermissionError("EM must acknowledge a BLOCKED transition before target date impact")
    action_item.state = ActionItemState.BLOCKED
    action_item.blocked_reason = blocked_reason
    action_item.target_date = estimated_unblock_date
    return action_item

Engineering Managers are responsible for the action item closure rate within their teams. A dashboard shows the current state of all open items across all postmortems, organised by owner and overdue status.
Action item SLA compliance is measured at the team, subject area, and organisation levels and reported in the quarterly learning review.
% of items closed by or before target date, measured at 30 days post-creation
Average calendar days from creation to closure, by category
% of open items currently past their target date
% of items that reached soft or hard escalation before closing
% of items that were closed without meeting completion criterion (retroactively invalidated)

Action item ownership requires a named individual owner, an acknowledgement, a completion criterion, and a target date. SLA targets are defined by category: Runbook (7 days), Detection (14 days), Process (21 days), Prevention (30 days). Items missing their target date transition to OVERDUE and trigger an escalation chain ending at director level. BLOCKED is distinct from OVERDUE and requires a named external dependency acknowledged by the Engineering Manager. Closure requires independent verification — the owner cannot self-certify completion.
Owner is always an individual, never a team
Completion criterion is written at creation time, not retroactively
BLOCKED requires a named external dependency and EM acknowledgement
OVERDUE items trigger escalation on days 3, 7, and at the hard threshold
Closure requires a reviewer (not the owner) to verify the completion criterion
Target metrics: > 80% on-time closure rate, < 10% overdue rate
